March 1, 2026

The Economic Outlook and Monetary Policy

Chair Jerome H. Powell

At the Brookings Institution, Washington, D.C.

Thank you for the opportunity to speak today. The Federal Open Market Committee remains committed to its dual mandate of maximum employment and price stability. Recent data on inflation and the labor market suggest that the disinflation process is continuing, though progress has been uneven. We will continue to make our monetary policy decisions on a meeting-by-meeting basis, guided by the totality of the data, the evolving outlook, and the balance of risks. Restoring price stability is essential to setting the stage for a period of strong labor market conditions that benefit all. The path forward is uncertain, and we remain prepared to adjust the stance of policy as appropriate. Thank you, and I look forward to your questions.
